title: [InstCombine] Strip leading zero indices from GEP
summary:
The developers generalized an optimization pattern by identifying a core simplification: removing leading zero indices from GEP instructions when the first index is a non-vector constant zero. Originally, the transformation handled only specific cases like `addrspacecast` patterns. The patch extends this to all GEPs by stripping leading zero indices when safe—specifically when the first index is a scalar constant zero and the first indexed type isn't a struct (since struct indices must be constants). This reduces GEP complexity and can enable further optimizations like load/store forwarding or constant folding.

The generalization is applied broadly across many test cases, including memcpy/memmove patterns, multi-dimensional array accesses, and various addrspace scenarios. It demonstrates how a targeted fix can evolve into a canonicalization that simplifies pointer arithmetic uniformly, improving codegen across diverse contexts without altering semantics. Developers should look for similar opportunities where a specific pattern reveals a generalizable property that can be applied to all matching IR constructs.
